steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Need to convert 310 ft²/hr to m²/min. Two conversions: feet→meters and hours→minutes.

steps.title: Solve
steps.content:
Area conversion (must square the linear factor): $1\text{ m} = 3.28\text{ ft}$, so $1\text{ m}^2 = (3.28)^2\text{ ft}^2 = 10.7584\text{ ft}^2$.
Time conversion: $1\text{ hr} = 60\text{ min}$.
$$310 \;\frac{\text{ft}^2}{\text{hr}} \times \frac{1\text{ m}^2}{10.7584\text{ ft}^2} \times \frac{1\text{ hr}}{60\text{ min}} = \frac{310}{10.7584 \times 60} \approx 0.48\text{ m}^2/\text{min}$$
